feed.title: SAP Community - Cloud Integration
feed.subtitle: Cloud Integration Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Authentication between SAP API Management and SAP Cloud Integration
entry.content:
Hello All,

What is the best practice to setup authentication between API Management and Cloud Integration in scenarios where APIM must trigger a CPI iFlow? 

I normally prefer using certificate based authentication by setting up a keystore in APIM and use that in the API provider that is configured for CPI. In CPI, I upload the public certificate of the keystore in the service key. However, we must purchase a certificate keypair signed by a authorised certificate authority or use a S-User generated certificate on SAP Passport. I am not aware if there is any other way to setup a key pair. Interested to know if someone can share their experience here.

I am aware that you can also use OAuth with CPI buy you must create policies inside API proxy to authenticate. Hence, I prefer the certificate based because there is no need to add additional policies in the proxy. I am curious to know how you setup this authentication in your projects?

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Process Integration test tool SAP PO usages for migration to BTP
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

As you know we have a tool called Process Integration test tool in SAP PO.

We are planning to migrate the interfaces from SAP PO to Cloud Integration capability of Integration Suite.

Does that tool can be used while regression testing of migrating interfaces from SAP PO to Cloud Integration?

May I know the replacement of such tool in Cloud Integration capability of Integration Suite?

 

Thanks,

Ramu.

entry.title: Receiver XI adapter in SAP CPI
entry.content:
Hi All,

Am trying a scenario, HTTP -->CPI-->S4(XI adapter). whenever am a sending a message from Postman, i could see two messages generated for one request from Postman and i can see only one message post to S4 System. In the screenshot i have entered correlation id, so it is displayed both the messages.

What could be the reason why it is generating two messages in CPI. Please share your suggestion.

Regards,

Mani

entry.title: Finding xml value based on attribute value in groovy script
entry.content:
Hi,

I have this payload

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<ReferenceInformation>
<AssignmentId>
<IdValue name="customid">JOB182741</IdValue>
<IdValue name="reference">1234XYZ</IdValue>
</AssignmentId>
</ReferenceInformation>
 
I want to find the IdValue where attribute name = "customid" and store that value in an exchange property
I created a groovyscript based on code snippets i found when searching but sofar i did not manage to make it work.
 
This is my groovyscript:
import com.sap.gateway.ip.core.customdev.util.Message;
import java.util.HashMap;
def Message processData(Message message) {
       def xml = message.getBody(String.class)      
       def completeXml = new XmlSlurper().parseText(xml)
       def netive_jobid = completeXml.ReferenceInformation.AssignmentId.IdValue[@name="customid"].text()
       message.setProperty("NTV_JOBID", netive_jobid)
       return message
}
But i get 2 errors
1)   unexpected token: = @ line 7, column 86.
2)   TypeError: Cannot convert undefined or null to object
 
I am not sure where i am going wrong. 
Any help is appreciated
 
Kr
Robert

entry.title: Error while trying to migrate interface object from SAP PO to Cloud Integration
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

While trying to migrate interface object from SAP PO to Cloud Integration using migration tool, I get the following error in SAP Integration Suite(Cloud Integration). I tried choosing different templates and the issue is not resolved and Could you please help us to resolve the issue, Thank you!

Regards

Ramu

entry.title: Import message mapping from SAP PO to Cloud Integration
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We are trying to import message mapping from SAP PO to Cloud Integration.

But, I get this error when trying to do so, could you help to know.

Can't we import the message mapping with parameterized mapping in to Cloud Integration?

I see there are parameters are being used in SAP, is that the cause of this issue while trying to migrate this mapping object from SAP PO to SAP Integration Suite(Cloud Integration)?

Thanks

Ramu

entry.title: SAP - Connectors give Invalid URL error when trying to call the API endpoint.
entry.content:
I'm implementing connector to our external system using SAP connectors. I have set up all the required fields including the base url. I have created and resource and I'm trying to try it executing, and getting this error,

{"requestId":"66050540e4b0579815d6b4a3","providerErrorCode":400,"message":"Bad Request","providerMessage":"<HTML><HEAD>
<TITLE>Invalid URL</TITLE>
</HEAD><BODY>
<H1>Invalid URL</H1>
The requested URL "http://%5bNo%20Host%5d/rest/310/OrderDetail", is invalid.<p>
Reference #9.974ddb17.1711605056.4c8c89fa
</BODY></HTML>
"}

I can notice that "Host" in the url, is this supposed to be resolved automatically ? Or is that the problem ? I mean when decoded this URL it becomes like this,

http://[No Host]/rest/310/OrderDetail?

I do not understand how this "Host" is resolved to my base url. Can someone please help ?

Note : I have some pre-request hooks to modify the headers as once of the server requirement.

 

 

Thanks.Dilan

entry.title: SAP Gateway ignored the filter pass by OData receiver adapter in Integration Sute
entry.content:
Dear All,

I am trying to implement an OData API in Integration Suite and having issues passing the filter to the backend S/4HANA system. This is an end-to-end OData service (Receiver and sender). The requirement is to publish an OData endpoint to expose an OData service built in S/4 HANA using Integration Suite.

Details are :

  1. S/4 HANA development environment has two clients 400 and 411. Cloud connector has access only to client 411. OData service was developed consuming a CDS view using Eclipse in client 400 and copied to 411.

Processing mode = Routing Based and added system alias

2. I tested the OData service with the following URL internally and it is giving the expected result.

https://<hostname>/sap/opu/odata/sap/ZAPI_O2_SALESORDERETA/SalesOrderETA/?$filter=(SoldToParty%20eq%20%2747474%27%20and%20OpenReqdDelivQtyInBaseUnit%20gt%200)

3. iFlow has been configured with the following information.

         a.Runtime configuration - Allowed Header(s) : CamelHttpQuery

          b. OData sender – System generated values.

        c. Odata sender -Script

I tried with following two options.

if(uriInfo.getFilter() != null){

        def filter = uriInfo.getFilter();

        if(odataURI.size()!=0)

        odataURI.append(urlDelimiter);

        odataURI.append("\$filter=").append(filter);

          log.logErrors(LogMessage.TechnicalError,  "Filter value:"+filterExpression);

         }

message.setHeader("odataEntity",entityName);

message.setHeader("odataURI",odataURI.toString());

When I do this, trace has following value for the filter.

odataURI                 : $filter=org.apache.olingo.odata2.core.uri.expression.FilterExpressionImpl@7d1f5f10

Then I tried with this

if(uriInfo.getFilter() != null){

             def filterExpression = uriInfo.getFilter().getExpressionString()

             odataURI.append(urlDelimiter);

        odataURI.append("\$filter=").append(filter);

         odataURI.append("\$filter=").append(filterExpression);

}

message.setHeader("odataEntity",entityName);

message.setHeader("odataURI",odataURI.toString());

Trace had the following value for the filter.

odataURI                 : $filter=(SoldToParty eq '47474' and OpenReqdDelivQtyInBaseUnit gt 0)

        d. OData receiver configuration looks like this.

Connection Address http://<hostname>:443/sap/opu/odata/sap/ZAPI_O2_SALESORDERETA

Proxy On-Premise

Authentication Basic

Credential Name – Populated with valid credential ID

Processing

Operation details : Query(GET)

Resource Path : SalesETA

HEADER DETAILS – Request Header = *

METADATA DETAILS – Customer query parameter = sap-client=411

The external endpoint is 

https://<hostname>.hana.ondemand.com/gw/odata/SAP/S4_SALESORDERETA;v=1/SalesOrderETATypeSet/?$filter=(SoldToParty eq '47474' and OpenReqdDelivQtyInBaseUnit gt 0)

Message log sys com.sap.gateway.core.ip.component.odata.exception.OsciException: Unauthorized : 401 : HTTP/1.1

It would be great if someone could help me.

Kind Regards

Shantha

entry.title: Integrate Solman and ServiceNow using SAP CPI or PO as middleware.
entry.content:
There is a requirement to Integrate Solman with ServiceNow using a middleware. SNMP Trap is being used at the Solman side. Is it possible to use SAP CPI or PO in this scenario as middleware?
And if SNMP Trap is avoided, is there any different way to send data to middleware?

Any information will be much appreciated.
Thank You.

entry.title: How to read MPL attachments from odata api as part of exception handling in SAP CI
entry.content:
Hi,

Scenario:

We are trying to post data to S4 OData api from sap CI and we want to test the exception handling part whenever there is failure in data posting to S4 . The error has to be captured and sent as a mail alert. 

I can see that CamelExceptionCaught contains error message as "Bad Request 400", but I need the actual error message, like "No delivery available for update".

In the OData Response body attachments i can see the actual error reason, but its not getting stored anywhere in the header or properties.

entry.title: SAP Cloud Integration Odata Adapter $metadata failed to connect to system error
entry.content:
Hi Gurus,

I am trying to configure receiver OData adapter (OData V2), I have added content modifier before request-reply --> Odata Adapter with header values APIKey = xxxxx.

Added header in Odata adapter under Header details for request and metadata.

While trying to Model Opertaion, I am getting error "failed to connect to system".

I tried with postman and http receiver adapter and it is working fine.

How do I fix connection issue while working on OData adapter.

URL = https://sandbox.api.sap.com/s4hanacloud/sap/opu/odata/sap/API_PURCHASEORDER_PROCESS_SRV/$metadata

BR,

RJ

entry.title: Restrict some IP (BTP data center IP) for Outbound Call from SAP Integration Suite
entry.content:
Hello,

This is the 2nd time I have encountered the same problem where receiving party is saying that they can whitelist up-to 6 IPs only. We are having SAP Integration Suite as middle ware where some data is being send from SAP to Receiving party via API/SFTP.

I have asked the same to SAP but as per them it is not possible to restrict IPs for outbound connection. Some time receiving party adjust and whitelist all the IPs but in case of they simply denied and we don't have any solution.

 

For the solution I have come across where as per the SAP note we can minimize the IP via cloud connector but not able to find how to do that. Can someone guide on this topic.

 

reference screenshot from SAP note : 3209308 

Thanks,

Aman Sharma

entry.title: Issue with EDI 856 to DELVRY07 IDOC mapping in CPI - IA - MAG
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am trying to create the MAG with the source as EDI 856 and target as DELVRY07 IDOC but unable to complete the MAG as not getting expected results.

EDI 856 Structure   

 HL*C^      ---> For each HL*C we need to create one E1EDL20.

      HL*O^   ---> each HL*C can have multiple HL*O^ and for each HL*O need to populate E1EDL24

         HL*T^  ---> Each HL*O can have multiple HL*T and for each HL*T need to populate E1EDl37.

              HL*P & HL*I  ---> each KL*T can have multiple HL*P & HL*I

The problem here is we can't pre transform the structure in MAG as there is no identifier in the source EDI file to establish the relationship based on that we can restructure.

Could you please advise or suggest how can we proceed further now ?

entry.title: Migration Assessment result is SMALL but no template available
entry.content:
I have an ICO in SAP PO that I want to migrate.

1 sender (SOAP XI Protocol), content based routing, 2 receivers, both receivers have a mapping, both receivers have a REST receiver channel  with XML to JSON conversion. Synchronous scenario.

Migration assessment indicates that this is going to be a small migration effort.

However when migrating this interface no template is available (only default).

This is how the assessment should work? In the end you have to migrate manually or use default.

entry.title: router Non-xml condition error as Invalid format of condition expression
entry.content:
Hello Experts, Cloud Integration , SAP Integration Suite,

I have stored properties in content modifier and trying to apply NON-XML condition in router to check the length.

But its giving an error as Invalid format of condition expression.

Property as below:

  1. C4C_ContactID --> value from Xpath
  2. userId --> value from Xpath
( ${property.C4C_ContactID} = null or ${property.C4C_ContactID.trim().length()} = '0' ) and ( ${property.userId} = null or ${property.userId.trim().length()} = '0' )

Kindly help me to understand what is wrong in above condition.

Thanks,

Ravi

entry.title: Integration possibilities from SAP CI(Integration Suite) to SWIFT???
entry.content:
Hi All,

While i was exploring the integration possibilities from CI to SWIFT had few questions around this as below.

1. If the integration has to be from S4HANA then there is a direct connector available for SWIFT which we can start configuring from SPRO tcode without the need of CI . Is is the best approach comparing to the other available options???

2. If the connector is not available might be S4HANA release issue or for normal ECC system then that case the traditional way of generating the file in AL11 from where CI will pick the drop onto the target system with all sort of security parameters(encryption/signature) maintained. We dont have standard prepackaged content like what we had for SWIFT SWCV in PI/PO??? Also the SFTP 3.0 which was used in PI/PO for SWIFT integration with the module configurations. How it can be accompanied in CI??? Are there any technical glitches???

3. Any other mechanisms for this integrations???

Please do share your thoughts on this.

Thank You.

Regards,

Farooq

entry.title: Event mesh S/4hana on-premise
entry.content:
hi,

I created a queue on the Event Mesh, how can I direct just one event to this queue? For example, when creating the Business Partner on S/4hana on-premise

I saw that it is necessary to insert the topic name of the queue but it doesn't work for me.

but it only works when I enter * but in this case it intercepts all events:

 

Does anyone know what might be missing?

thank you

entry.title: Removing NULL from SAP CPI mapping - context
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

Please suggest how can I remove the NULL from the context ? tried couple of script but none working.

entry.title: Same Mapping Different WSDL
entry.content:
Dear Integration fellows,

I have developed an extension of IDoc and will upload it into CPI, so that the IDOC is getting recognized when sending over CPI. However, when replacing the old wsdl with the new in Graphical Mapping in CPI, all the mappings I have done are marked as deprecated and cannot be used. 

This means I have to do all the mapping from begining. Is there a way to avoid this and copy the mapping to the new wsdl structure?

entry.title: SAP Event Mesh Service Key Creation failed
entry.content:
Hi All,
Last week i created an SAP Event Mesh Instance based on this tutorial, https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/cp-enterprisemessaging-instance-create.html.

To generate this instance i used the following JSON-File based on the service descriptor that SAP offers in the Help Section.

Instance Creation JSON:

Unfortunately when creating the OAuth2.0 service Keys (for AMQP / MQTT & HTTPREST) for external connection it didnt work out. 


the JSON I used:

The System tells me that he cannot find messaging and management.

Based on the tutorial and on this blog here I choose to leave all the placeholders as delivered from the Tutorial but it still doesnt work: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/service-key-creation-for-event-mesh/qaq-p/12527257  

Can anyone Help me with this Issue?

Thanks
